free_text: Investigation of proton induced resonance reactions on
Two types of targets have been used; a natural KI
target for the measurement of the yield curve and a
natural KCL target for the energy and strength
calibration and the angular distribution measurements.
The targets consist of a 10 mu-g/cm2 carbon backing on
which the potassium salt has been evaporated in vacuum.
The thickness of the target was measured as 3.2 keV at
the 2.54 MeV resonance.
Data digitizing error
COMMON section and REACTION records in
Sub 003,004 were changed.
Data are given in counts per 300 microC.
Semiconductor detectors with the thicknesses of 20
mu-m and 300 mu-m (active area of 25 mm2) were used
separately and as a dE-E counter telescope.
System has been used to reduce the background
arising from proton pile-up. Detectors were placed at
a distance of 8.5 cm from the target.

110, 125, 140, 149 and 155 degr. is presented.
